input.title: Invisible Labor
input.description: A documentary following three domestic workers in a major city over two years — a nanny, a home health aide, and a house cleaner — exploring the invisible economy of care work and the systematic exclusion of domestic workers from standard labor protections.
input.fund_priorities: ITVS supports independent documentary films that illuminate the lives and experiences of underrepresented communities for public media audiences.
input.in_place: Director and producer attached, all three subjects confirmed with 6 months of footage already shot, fiscal sponsor confirmed

input.title: Night Shift
input.description: A short documentary about overnight workers in a 24-hour city — who keeps things running while everyone sleeps.

input.title: Border Languages
input.description: A documentary about the disappearing minority languages spoken in Europe's border regions — Alsatian in France, Sorbian in Germany, Aromanian in Greece — and the communities fighting to keep them alive. The film follows three families raising children in languages that schools no longer teach and governments no longer officially support.
input.fund_priorities: Creative Europe MEDIA supports projects that promote European cultural diversity, cross-border cooperation, and the circulation of European audiovisual works.
input.in_place: Director attached (French-German dual national), one family in each country confirmed for preliminary access, University of Strasbourg linguistic advisor as consultant
input.social_case: UNESCO classifies all three languages as endangered. The film directly addresses European cultural diversity and cross-border heritage — core Creative Europe priorities.

input.title: The Seed Keepers
input.description: A documentary following women seed custodians in three East African countries who maintain indigenous crop varieties against the spread of commercial monocultures. The film explores food sovereignty, colonial agricultural legacies, and the quiet resistance of keeping seeds alive.
input.fund_priorities: JustFilms supports documentary films and new media that address inequality, strengthen democratic values, and advance human rights.
input.in_place: Director and producer attached, access confirmed with seed custodians in three communities, 3 months of footage shot, university agronomist as consultant

input.title: Untitled
input.description: A film about people and their stories. Real people, real stories. Showing the world as it really is.
input.fund_priorities: The Sundance Documentary Fund supports non-fiction films on contemporary themes that exhibit originality, artistic risk, and a strong potential for social impact.

input.title: The Tiffin Line
input.description: A documentary tracing a single day in the dabbawala lunch delivery network of a major Indian city — from suburban kitchens where lunches are prepared at dawn, through the sorting stations and commuter trains, to office desks across the city. The film uses the logistics of 200,000 daily deliveries to explore class, labor, urban infrastructure, and the economics of feeding a megacity.
input.fund_priorities: The IDFA Bertha Fund supports creative documentaries from Africa, Asia, the Middle East, Latin America, and Eastern Europe that combine artistic ambition with social relevance.
input.in_place: Director attached, preliminary access to one dabbawala cooperative confirmed, urban logistics researcher as consultant

input.title: Radio Selva
input.description: A documentary about indigenous community radio stations in the Amazon basin that broadcast in languages with fewer than 5,000 speakers. The stations serve as news source, emergency warning system, cultural archive, and political organizing tool for communities with no internet access and limited mobile coverage.
input.funder: Tribeca Film Institute Latin America Fund
input.fund_priorities: Supports Latin American documentary filmmakers whose work addresses social justice, cultural preservation, and underrepresented perspectives.
input.in_place: Director and sound designer attached, access confirmed to three radio stations across two countries, 2 months of footage and audio recordings, linguistic anthropologist as consultant
input.social_case: UNESCO has classified all three broadcast languages as severely endangered. The radio stations are the only media in these languages.
